gap_summaries: [ultrathink] CRITICAL: new_run_status seeded every node PENDING regardless of mode — out-of-mode nodes should be SKIPPED so status counts and downstream invariants stay honest. FIXED inline in Task 7 (new_run_status now marks node SKIPPED iff status.mode not in node.modes; added test_new_run_status_marks_out_of_mode_nodes_skipped).

[ultrathink] CRITICAL: runnable_nodes took mode= as a separate parameter while RunStatus already records status.mode — two-source-of-truth footgun where a test or caller could pass an inconsistent mode and silently mis-schedule. FIXED inline in Tasks 10-13 (runnable_nodes now derives mode from status.mode; all test call sites updated; topological_order still takes mode explicitly because it is a policy-shape function independent of any run).

[ultrathink] CRITICAL: status_from_dict accepted any string for mode on reload, meaning a hand-edited or corrupted status file with mode=midfield would silently corrupt scheduler behavior. FIXED inline in Task 7 (status_from_dict validates mode against _VALID_MODES and raises ValueError on mismatch; new_run_status applies the same check on construction; added test_new_run_status_rejects_unknown_mode).

[ultrathink] ARCHITECTURAL: scheduler does not enforce state-transition legality — a phase-runner that marks a gated node COMPLETE without going through AWAITING_APPROVAL would not be stopped by W0-M01. INTENTIONAL design (the scheduler is a pure read of what-is-runnable-next, not a state-machine guardian); the constraint belongs to W0-M02 phase-runner and W0-M03 approval-gate. DOCUMENTED as Design Decision #9 in the plan header so reviewers do not mistake omission for oversight.
[ultrathink] IMPORTANT: no test for json.JSONDecodeError to PolicyError translation. FIXED inline in Task 3 (added test_invalid_json_input_raises_policy_error + test_top_level_non_object_raises).

[ultrathink] IMPORTANT: Task 9 placed `import json` at the bottom of tests/test_lifecycle_status.py with a noqa-E402 hack. FIXED inline in Task 7 (imports consolidated at the top of the file when Task 7 first edits it; bottom-of-file import + noqa removed).

[ultrathink] IMPORTANT: _validate_acyclic loop carried a misleading comment (dep is guaranteed to be in nodes) in a loop that did not use the dep variable, plus an O(deps) increment instead of a single len(deps) assignment. FIXED inline in Task 5 (refactored to in_degree[node.id] = len(node.deps) with an accurate comment about where the closed-world invariant is actually used).

[ultrathink] IMPORTANT: Design Decision #6 originally claimed a default artifact_exists implementation exists; no such default is provided in the code (the parameter is required). FIXED inline (Design Decision #6 now says the caller — phase-runner in W0-M02 — constructs the appropriate callable; no default).
[ultrathink] IMPORTANT: no test for empty active-set scheduling (mode filter eliminates all nodes). FIXED inline in Task 10 (added test_topo_order_empty_active_set_returns_empty_list).

[ultrathink] MINOR (deliberately deferred, kept as-is): version:int accepts 0/negative; gate_decision is str|None without enum; ArtifactRecord.sha256 defaults to empty string; save_status output is not byte-stable across re-runs (only structurally equal); track/agent_role/verifier/validator_skill fields are free-form (extensible by design); interactive is permissive-truthy via bool(); full-both-modes.policy.json was listed in File Structure but no task consumes it (Note 8 documents this honestly); _VALID_MODES duplicated across lifecycle_policy.py and lifecycle_scheduler.py (acceptable — 1-line each). 11 total minor items kept as-is.

[ultrathink] DEFERRED (explicit non-goals of W0-M01, documented in the plan Scope section): phase-runner (W0-M02), verifier registry (W0-M02), approval-gate primitive (W0-M03), entry-mode router CLI (W0-M04), core/lifecycle_events.py (W0-M02), CLI status command (W0-M03/M04), multi-component policies (W10), WDS track integration (W9). 8 total deferred items.
